body.title: Detect and recover orphaned ollama runner processes after OOM cascade
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
On 2026-04-27 the OOM killer fired twice in 8 seconds, killing both llama runners. Ollama's restart logic spawned PID 237776 (qwen2.5:7b) but lost the handle to it — the process ran at 392% CPU for 24+ hours until manually killed. ollama ps never listed it. This is an ollama bug we can't fix, but we can detect and recover from it.

Fix: add a periodic Igor habit (PROC_OLLAMA_ORPHAN_CHECK, schedule ~300s) that: (1) queries ollama /api/ps for tracked runners, (2) counts actual ollama runner processes via pgrep, (3) if count(pgrep) > count(/api/ps) + 1, log a WARNING and trigger an ollama service restart via the sudo relay (sudo systemctl restart ollama). The restart is the safe recovery — ollama reloads cleanly, tracked runners resume, orphans die.

Also: when any ollama call returns a 500, log the full response body at WARNING level so the oversized input that triggered it is visible in the logs.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/embedder.py or a new wild_igor/igor/habits/ollama_watchdog.py (PROC_OLLAMA_ORPHAN_CHECK habit), wild_igor/igor/cognition/reasoners/ollama_reasoner.py (log 500 response body)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code. No new memory schemas. Sudo relay already exists at ~/.TheIgors/sudo_relay/ — use it.
**Scope boundary:** IN: orphan detection habit + 500 logging. OUT: fixing the OOM root cause (that's T-ollama-input-cap), GPU procurement, kernel OOM tuning.
**Test plan:** Unit test: mock pgrep returning count > api/ps count, assert restart is triggered via sudo relay. Run: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q -k 'ollama_watch or orphan'
